data.title: Earnings reports on August 1, 2025: TELUS expected to report $0.17/share on $3.68B revenue, Exxon Mobil at $1.53/share on $81.31B revenue.
data.description: Several companies are expected to report earnings before the bell, including Brookfield Business, TELUS, Anglogold Ashanti, IES Holdings, EchoStar, CNH Industrial, Church & Dwight Co, Patria Investments, Newell Brands, Liberty Global, Kimberly-Clark, Colgate-Palmolive, Avient, Exxon Mobil, RBC Bearings, Regeneron Pharmaceuticals, Perella Weinberg, Piper Sandler, and nVent Electric. Key estimates include TELUS reporting earnings of $0.17 per share on revenue of $3.68 billion, Anglogold Ashanti reporting earnings of $1.30 per share on revenue of $2.32 billion, and Exxon Mobil reporting earnings of $1.53 per share on revenue of $81.31 billion. The full story is available on Benzinga.com.

data.title: ADFW 2025 to double in size at new location in Abu Dhabi under the patronage of His Highness Sheikh Khaled bin Mohamed bin Zayed Al Nahyan.
data.description: Taking place in Abu Dhabi, UAE on Aug. 1, 2025, under the patronage of His Highness Sheikh Khaled bin Mohamed bin Zayed Al Nahyan, the Crown Prince of Abu Dhabi and Chairman of the Executive Council, the upcoming Abu Dhabi Finance Week (ADFW) is set to return as the largest financial event in the region. This event will bring together industry leaders, experts, and participants from the financial sector to discuss and showcase the latest trends and innovations in finance. Stay tuned for a week full of insightful discussions, networking opportunities, and valuable insights into the financial landscape.

data.title: "Exhibition launches marking China's 20th ecological milestone. Beijing International Club hosts global dialogue. #BetterChina2025"
data.description: Opening on July 28-29 at Beijing International Club, the "Song of the Earth | Artistic Documentary for a Better China 2025" exhibition series celebrates the 20th anniversary of China's ecological development concept. This event marks a pivotal moment as the country gears up for the upcoming years.

data.title: Moderna (NASDAQ: MRNA) to release Q2 earnings, expected loss $2.97 per share, revenue $112.96 million. Shares down 8.1%.
data.description: Moderna, Inc. is set to announce its second-quarter earnings after the market closes on Friday, August 1. Analysts predict the Massachusetts-based company will post a loss of $2.97 per share, an improvement from the $3.33 loss per share reported a year earlier. Revenue for the quarter is expected to be around $112.96 million, down from $241 million in the same period last year. In response to declining COVID-19 vaccine sales and market volatility, Moderna plans to cut its global workforce by 10% by the end of the year. Following this announcement, Moderna's stock price dropped by 8.1% to close at $29.56 on Thursday. For more insights, readers can refer to the latest analyst ratings on the Analyst Stock Ratings page.

data.title: Government of Odisha partners with GFTN to create FinTech Hub in Bhubaneswar; NUS-AIDF offers FinTech course for Odisha students.
data.description: Odisha-based students now have the opportunity to apply for the Certificate in FinTech & InsurTech program offered by the National University of Singapore - Asian Institute of Digital Finance (NUS-AIDF). Applications for this program opened on August 1, 2025. The initiative, a collaboration between the Government of Odisha and the Global Finance & Technology Institute, aims to provide students with essential skills in financial technology and insurance technology. Interested individuals in Bhubaneswar, India, can now submit their applications for this program.

data.title: Li Auto delivered 30,731 vehicles in July 2025, bringing total cumulative deliveries to 1,368,541.
data.description: Li Auto Inc., a prominent player in China's new energy vehicle sector, disclosed its latest delivery figures. In July 2025, the company successfully delivered 30,731 vehicles. As of July 31, 2025, Li Auto has achieved an impressive milestone, with a total of 1,368,541 vehicles delivered.

data.title: BLUETTI launched Apex 300 after raising $5.4M, leading crowdfunding charts. Flagship portable power station now in retail.
data.description: In Frankfurt, Germany, on August 1, 2025, BLUETTI, a prominent provider of clean energy solutions, revealed the launch of its top product, the Apex 300 Portable Power Station. This came after a successful fundraising campaign on Indiegogo in 2025, where they secured a remarkable $5.4 million in funding, marking it as one of the most funded campaigns of the year.

data.title: Wall Street predicts Chevron (NYSE: CVX) will report Q2 earnings of $1.75/share and revenue of $45.59B, down from previous year. Chevron shares fell 1.3% on Thursday. Exxon Mobil lost dispute over Hess Corp's offshore oil assets in Guyana.
data.description: Chevron Corporation (CVX) is set to announce its second-quarter earnings before the opening bell on Aug. 1. Analysts forecast the Houston-based company to post quarterly earnings of $1.75 per share, a decrease from $2.55 per share in the same period last year. Revenue for the quarter is anticipated to be around $45.59 billion, down from $51.18 billion a year earlier. In recent news, Chevron emerged victorious in a dispute with Exxon Mobil over offshore oil assets in Guyana owned by Hess Corp (HESS). Exxon CEO Darren Woods confirmed this development during a CNBC interview on July 18. On Thursday, Chevron's stock closed at $151.64, marking a 1.3% decline. For more details and analyst ratings, interested readers can access the complete story on Benzinga's website.
